name: London Ambulance Service
description:
The London Ambulance Service dispatches ambulances in emergencies. 
    The key goal is to allocate an available ambulance for every call that can reach the scene within 11 minutes. 
    The entire dispatch process must not exceed a set maximum time limit.

    To make this work, the computer system (CAD) analyzes incident forms and assigns vehicles. 
    It is vital to track the exact location of moving ambulances. 
    This requires staff to follow standard routes and correctly communicate departure and destination, 
    allowing radio operators and the CAD to record data accurately.
highLevelGoals: Allocate an available ambulance for every emergency call to ensure it reaches the scene within 11 minutes, while adhering to the maximum dispatch time limit.
Facilitate effective communication between the radio operator and the ambulance crew to ensure accurate tracking of ambulance locations and status during dispatch.
Provide the ambulance crew with all relevant incident details and updates to enable effective medical assistance at the scene of emergencies.
Ensure the CAD system is functioning correctly to support efficient dispatch operations and accurate data recording.
Ensure that emergency call handlers input incident details accurately into the CAD system to facilitate proper dispatching and resource allocation.
Utilize the CAD system to determine the most efficient routes for ambulances to minimize response times and maximize resource utilization.
Prioritize the safety and well-being of patients during emergencies by ensuring timely and effective medical assistance is provided.
Optimize the allocation of ambulances and crews to ensure that resources are used effectively and efficiently during emergency responses.
lowLevelGoals: Query the system for a list of currently available ambulances to respond to an emergency call.
Automatically select the closest available ambulance to the incident location for dispatch.
Send a dispatch command to the selected ambulance to respond to the emergency call.
Request the current GPS location of the dispatched ambulance to monitor its progress towards the incident.
Send a status update from the ambulance crew to the system indicating their current location and estimated time of arrival.
Fetch the incident details from the CAD system to provide to the ambulance crew before they arrive at the scene.
Enter the details of the emergency call into the CAD system to ensure accurate record-keeping and dispatching.
Use the CAD system to calculate the most efficient route for the ambulance to take to the incident location.
Query the system to check the availability of medical supplies on the ambulance before dispatching.
Query the system to check the availability of personnel on the ambulance before dispatching.
